decisions.rationale: The gap consists of a comma and a space, which are punctuation used to separate clauses and do not contain any assertive semantic content.
The gap consists of a comma and a space, which are punctuation used to separate the main clause from a limiting clause and do not contain any assertive semantic content.
The gap consists of a period, which is terminal punctuation and does not contain any assertive semantic content.
The gap consists of a comma and a space, which are punctuation used to separate the main prediction from a qualifying assumption and do not contain any assertive semantic content.
The gap consists of a period, which is terminal punctuation and does not contain any assertive semantic content.

The gap contains a comma and the conjunction 'or', which serves to connect two alternative falsification conditions. This connector is not independently assertive, as the two adjacent clauses are fully captured as claims.
The gap consists of a period, which is terminal punctuation and does not contain any assertive semantic content.
The gap consists of the conjunction 'and', which connects two observable metrics. The connector itself is not independently assertive, as the two connected items are fully captured as claims.
The gap consists of a period, which is terminal punctuation and does not contain any assertive semantic content.
